branches.label: RBI & Monetary Policy
branches.detail: Covers the Reserve Bank of India's role as India's central bank and the tools it uses to control money supply and inflation — a near-guaranteed source of questions in every banking exam.

children.label: RBI Functions
children.detail: The core responsibilities assigned to RBI under the RBI Act, 1934.

children.label: Note Issue
children.detail: RBI's sole authority to issue currency notes in India (except the ₹1 note, issued by the Finance Ministry).

children.label: Banker's Bank
children.detail: RBI's role in regulating and lending to commercial banks, and managing their reserves.

children.label: Policy Tools
children.detail: The instruments RBI uses to control liquidity and inflation in the economy.

children.label: Repo Rate
children.detail: The rate at which RBI lends short-term funds to commercial banks — the primary tool for controlling inflation.

children.label: Reverse Repo
children.detail: The rate at which RBI borrows funds from commercial banks, used to absorb excess liquidity.

children.label: CRR & SLR
children.detail: Cash Reserve Ratio and Statutory Liquidity Ratio — the minimum reserves banks must maintain with RBI and in liquid assets respectively.

branches.label: Types of Banks
branches.detail: The structure of India's banking system across ownership and purpose — a frequent source of classification-style questions.

children.label: Commercial Banks
children.detail: Banks that accept deposits and lend for profit, the largest segment of India's banking system.

children.label: Public Sector
children.detail: Banks where the government holds majority ownership, e.g. SBI, PNB, Bank of Baroda.

children.label: Private Sector
children.detail: Banks owned primarily by private shareholders, e.g. HDFC, ICICI, Axis Bank.

children.label: Cooperative Banks
children.detail: Member-owned banks regulated jointly by RBI and state governments.

children.label: Urban
children.detail: Cooperative banks serving urban and semi-urban customers.

children.label: Rural
children.detail: Cooperative banks, including PACS, serving agricultural and rural credit needs.

children.label: Payment Banks
children.detail: A restricted banking license allowing deposits and payments but not lending.

children.label: Examples
children.detail: Airtel Payments Bank, India Post Payments Bank, and Fino Payments Bank are common examples asked in exams.

branches.label: Govt Schemes
branches.detail: Central government financial inclusion and social security schemes implemented through the banking system — frequently tested with launch year and objective.

children.label: Financial Inclusion
children.detail: Schemes aimed at bringing unbanked populations into the formal banking system.

children.label: Jan Dhan Yojana
children.detail: A scheme providing zero-balance bank accounts to promote universal banking access, launched in 2014.

children.label: Atal Pension Yojana
children.detail: A pension scheme for unorganized-sector workers guaranteeing a fixed monthly pension after age 60.

children.label: Insurance
children.detail: Low-premium government-backed insurance schemes distributed via banks.

children.label: PMJJBY
children.detail: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana — a low-premium life insurance scheme for ages 18-50.

children.label: PMSBY
children.detail: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana — a low-premium accidental death and disability insurance scheme.

branches.label: Banking Terms
branches.detail: Common terminology and acronyms tested directly or used within case-based banking exam questions.

children.label: NPA
children.detail: Non-Performing Asset — a loan where the borrower has stopped repaying principal or interest for over 90 days.

children.label: Gross NPA
children.detail: The total value of all non-performing loans on a bank's books before any provisioning.

children.label: Net NPA
children.detail: Gross NPA minus the provisions a bank has already set aside for expected losses.

children.label: Digital Banking
children.detail: Electronic methods of transferring money, increasingly central to banking exam current affairs.

children.label: NEFT
children.detail: National Electronic Funds Transfer — a batch-processed electronic transfer system with no minimum/maximum limit.

children.label: RTGS
children.detail: Real Time Gross Settlement — used for high-value transfers (minimum ₹2 lakh), processed individually and instantly.

children.label: UPI
children.detail: Unified Payments Interface — a real-time mobile payment system enabling instant bank-to-bank transfers.

branches.label: International Bodies
branches.detail: Global financial institutions whose roles and India-related facts are commonly tested in static GK sections of banking exams.

children.label: IMF
children.detail: International Monetary Fund — promotes global monetary cooperation and financial stability.

children.label: Role
children.detail: Provides financial assistance and policy advice to member countries facing balance-of-payment problems.

children.label: World Bank
children.detail: An international institution providing loans and grants to developing countries for development projects.

children.label: Role
children.detail: Funds infrastructure, education, and poverty-reduction projects, distinct from the IMF's monetary-stability focus.
